claim: "Rocks falling into the ocean" is a factor that contributes to observable sea level rise on human time scales.

result.claim: "Rocks falling into the ocean" is a factor that contributes to observable sea level rise on human time scales.
result.reasoning:
The searches for "rocks falling into ocean sea level rise", "mass displacement sea level rise", "geological processes sea level rise", and "landslides sea level rise" did not yield any direct evidence to support the claim that "rocks falling into the ocean" is a factor contributing to observable sea level rise on human time scales. While some results mentioned shoreline erosion and landslides as geological processes, they did not establish a link to a measurable contribution to global sea level rise within human timescales. The available propositions and chunks focused on other aspects of sea level change (e.g., shoreline dynamics, climate change impacts) or unrelated topics.
